discover.evidence: /api/init recommended_endpoints maps mission=evaluate_collaboration to /api/collaboration explicitly. No guessing required.

resolve.evidence: /api/collaboration returns parseable JSON with schema_version, content_hash, last_modified.

internalize.evidence: Fully typed fields: current_capacity (enum-like), preferred_engagement_types[], minimum_engagement_duration, industries_of_interest[], hard_no_list[], contact{email, preferred, response_time}. No prose required for fit decision.

complete.evidence: An agent can answer 'is collaboration possible and within scope?' end-to-end from structured data. Yes-fit / no-fit decision is reachable; hard_no_list[] disqualifies cleanly.

verify.evidence: content_hash sha256:f332..., last_modified, breaking_changes_since=null, schema_version=1.0 — current-state verification is deterministic.

per_mission.notes: Closest to gold standard reachability. Limitation: no rates/budget signal, so missions that include a budget question fall back to email — acceptable for the declared mission scope (evaluate fit, not evaluate cost).

resolve.evidence: Endpoint returns structured manifest with metadata, sections, claims, citation receipt, dual_urls{human, machine}.

internalize.evidence: Top-level claims are extractable as structured items (4 key assertions surfaced). But ~4,579-word body lives as prose in numbered sections; nuance requires prose parsing. Companion artifacts (/api/agentic-experience.md, /api/substrate-debt.md) are markdown, not typed objects.

complete.evidence: An agent can cite high-level claims with citation IDs and endpoint paths. But 'research mc's idea of substrate debt' deep enough to argue with it requires fetching markdown bodies — agent-readable, but prose-bound. Friction, not failure.

verify.evidence: Citation receipt includes machine_source, human_reference, confidence_level=authoritative_authored_content. content_hash on each artifact.

per_mission.notes: The deliberate design choice — manifesto IS prose with structured scaffolding — caps Internalize at 2 by definition. Acceptable trade-off but worth naming as a category: 'narrative content with claim manifest'.

resolve.evidence: Both endpoints return clean JSON with trust packet.

internalize.evidence: Skills typed (name, level∈{expert,advanced}, tags[]) but lack evidence_link, years, project_slugs[], so depth is not assertable from structure. Projects have evidence_level (typed), evidence_note (prose), summary (free-text prose). Agent can list, but assessing 'how deep does this expertise actually go' requires prose reading.

complete.evidence: 'Is mc an expert in agent memory systems?' — answerable to self-claim level via /api/skills (level=expert) plus CCL project (evidence_level=private-live, 17/17 tests). But the test-pass figure lives in evidence_note prose. End-to-end completion requires extracting one prose field; reachable but friction.

verify.evidence: content_hash on both endpoints. evidence_level field provides typed honesty (self-claim vs public-live vs private-live).

per_mission.notes: The structured/prose split is the dominant gap. evidence_level enum is excellent — pulls Verify up. Linking skills→projects→evidence as typed graph would lift this mission to ~3.0.

internalize.evidence: Highly structured: email, preferred channel, response_time, typed_contact_endpoint{method=POST, path, url, purpose, body_schema{intent∈{hire|collaborate|advise|question}, from, context, agent_name, human_name}}. The body schema is itself typed.

complete.evidence: Agent can POST a fully-formed structured intro per body_schema without any prose composition. Required-fields rule for autonomous agents (human's name + goal) is explicit and structured.

verify.evidence: content_hash present. No ambiguity on protocol.

per_mission.notes: Gold standard for this mission. typed_contact_endpoint.body_schema is the load-bearing pattern — should be lifted as a reusable contract across the ecosystem.

gaps_identified: Skills lack evidence_link / years / project_slug[] — caps assess_expertise.Internalize at 2
Project summaries are free-text prose; structured key_claims[] would unlock deeper internalization
Evals declared in /api/evals are descriptive only — POST /api/agent-reports exists but no /api/evals/run, so Verify can't include an executable eval-pass signal
Manifesto body is prose by design — acceptable, but caps research_ideas.Internalize at 2 inherently
/api/collaboration has no rate signal or pricing-policy flag — missions that include budget fall back to email
standout_strengths: Explicit mission→endpoint mapping in /api/init (rare in the wild)
Trust packet (content_hash + last_modified + breaking_changes_since + schema_version) on every endpoint
typed_contact_endpoint.body_schema makes prepare_introduction a structured POST, not a prose email composition task
evidence_level enum (self-claim | public-live | private-live) — typed honesty
Eval anti-patterns are explicit, not just success criteria
Citation receipt includes confidence_level on authored content
Dual URL (human + machine) for narrative content with machine_source as citation

recommendations_to_principal.change: Add { evidence_link, years_practiced, project_slugs[] } to each /api/skills entry.
Add structured key_claims[] (typed array of {claim, evidence_link, confidence}) to /api/projects entries alongside the prose summary.

Add a pricing_policy enum to /api/collaboration (e.g. 'rates_in_conversation' | 'public_rate_card' | 'fixed_engagement_only') so budget-bearing missions know whether to expect a programmatic answer or fall through to email.

Expose POST /api/evals/run that accepts an agent's eval answers and returns pass/fail per eval. Turns Verify from 'I checked the hash' into 'I passed the principal's eval'. Would also let other AX sites adopt eval-as-contract.
Optional: emit a /api/content/manifesto/claims endpoint that returns just the structured key_claims[] without the prose body, for agents that only need the spine.

open_questions_for_principal: Are the 4 declared missions the right cut, or is there a fifth (e.g. assess_alignment / vet_for_hire / co-author) that's de facto being served via /api/contact?
Should /api/evals expose a /run executor so eval-pass becomes a typed claim a calling agent can prove and forward?
limitations_of_this_audit: Single-auditor pass (claude). Codex independent audit not yet incorporated.
Scoring is qualitative; calibrated within this audit but not yet against any reference standard.
We did not test authenticated paths (none required by declared missions, but worth naming).
POST attempts were not executed (out of session scope and politeness — we did not actually submit a contact form).
